title: CRIS: Cross-Constraint Repair Safety Analysis for Scalable Multi-Constraint Data Cleaning
description: We propose CRIS, a framework that formally analyzes the interaction structure between heterogeneous data quality constraints (functional dependencies, conditional functional dependencies, denial constraints) by constructing a Constraint Interaction Graph (CIG) that captures repair safety relationships. The CIG enables decomposition of the multi-constraint repair problem into maximally independent subproblems, achieving near-holistic repair quality while providing speedups proportional to the interaction sparsity of the constraint set.
motivation: Real-world data cleaning and integration scenarios require enforcing multiple heterogeneous constraint types simultaneously. Current approaches either repair each constraint type independently (fast but introduces cascading violations as repairs for one constraint break another) or treat all constraints holistically in a single optimization (e.g., HoloClean's probabilistic inference), which is accurate but computationally expensive. The key gap is that the interaction structure between constraints is neither analyzed nor exploited: in practice, most constraint pairs are non-interfering (their repairs cannot conflict), yet holistic methods pay the cost of joint reasoning over all constraints. No existing work formally characterizes cross-constraint repair safety or uses it for principled decomposition.
proposed_approach: CRIS performs three-level repair safety analysis between constraint pairs: (1) Structural safety -- syntactic check on attribute overlap between repair targets and constraint references, (2) Predicate-aware safety -- semantic analysis of predicate entailment to identify compatible repairs, (3) Data-dependent safety -- empirical sampling to estimate interference rates with a threshold epsilon=0.01 justified by binomial confidence intervals and validated via sensitivity analysis over epsilon in {0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.02, 0.05}. These safety relationships are encoded in a Constraint Interaction Graph (CIG), which is then decomposed: independent components are repaired in parallel, DAG components are repaired in topological order, and cyclic components use minimum feedback arc set decomposition with verification passes. We prove that when the CIG is acyclic and individual repairs produce unique minimum-change solutions, the decomposed repair is equivalent to monolithic repair. CRIS works as a meta-algorithm composing existing single-constraint repair tools.

related_work.title: Holistic Data Cleaning: Putting Violations Into Context
related_work.authors: Xu Chu, Ihab F. Ilyas, Paolo Papotti
related_work.relation: First to encode heterogeneous constraints in a single conflict hypergraph, but does not analyze which constraints actually interact -- all are treated as potentially interacting. CRIS explicitly identifies safe pairs and exploits sparsity.

related_work.title: HoloClean: Holistic Data Repairs with Probabilistic Inference
related_work.authors: Theodoros Rekatsinas, Xu Chu, Ihab F. Ilyas, Christopher Re
related_work.relation: Unifies constraint-based and statistical cleaning via probabilistic inference. Achieves high quality but scales poorly with constraint set size. CRIS can use HoloClean as a subroutine for small dense subproblems while handling decomposition efficiently.

related_work.title: Horizon: Scalable Dependency-driven Data Cleaning
related_work.authors: El Kindi Rezig, Mourad Ouzzani, Walid G. Aref, Ahmed K. Elmagarmid, Ahmed R. Mahmoud, Michael Stonebraker
related_work.relation: Achieves linear-time FD repair by leveraging intra-FD interaction patterns. Handles only FDs. CRIS generalizes to cross-type interactions between FDs, CFDs, and DCs.

related_work.title: Topology-Aware Subset Repair via Entropy-Guided Density and Graph Decomposition
related_work.authors: Guoqi Zhao, Xixian Han, Xiaolong Wan
related_work.relation: Decomposes conflict graphs for CFD repair into independent subgraphs. CRIS differs by analyzing interactions between different constraint types (cross-type) rather than within a single type.

related_work.title: AlphaClean: Automatic Generation of Data Cleaning Pipelines
related_work.relation: Searches for good cleaning pipeline orderings via generate-then-search. CRIS provides formal analysis of why certain orderings are better based on repair safety, and can prune AlphaClean's search space.

related_work.title: SAGA: A Scalable Framework for Optimizing Data Cleaning Pipelines for Machine Learning Applications
related_work.authors: Shafaq Siddiqi, Roman Kern, Matthias Boehm
related_work.relation: Generates top-K cleaning pipelines using Auto-ML ideas. Focuses on downstream ML performance. CRIS optimizes for data quality directly with theoretical safety guarantees.

related_work.title: Computing Optimal Repairs for Functional Dependencies
related_work.authors: Ester Livshits, Benny Kimelfeld, Sudeepa Roy
related_work.relation: Analyzes complexity of optimal FD repair (NP-hard). CRIS's decomposition reduces effective problem size for each subproblem, making approximation more tractable.

related_work.title: BigDansing: A System for Big Data Cleansing
related_work.authors: Zuhair Khayyat, Ihab F. Ilyas, Alekh Jindal, Samuel Madden, Mourad Ouzzani, Paolo Papotti, Jorge-Arnulfo Quiane-Ruiz, Nan Tang, Si Yin
related_work.relation: Scalable data cleaning with logical plan of operators. Optimizes individual rule execution but does not analyze cross-rule interactions for decomposition.

related_work.title: Discovering Denial Constraints
related_work.authors: Xu Chu, Ihab F. Ilyas, Paolo Papotti
related_work.relation: Provides the DC discovery algorithms needed to obtain the constraint sets that CRIS operates on.

related_work.title: Auto-Test: Learning Semantic-Domain Constraints for Unsupervised Error Detection in Tables
related_work.authors: Qixu Chen, Yeye He, Raymond Chi-Wing Wong, et al.
related_work.relation: Introduces semantic-domain constraints as a new constraint class. CRIS's framework can incorporate these as additional constraint types in the interaction analysis.

related_work.title: Messing Up with BART: Error Generation for Evaluating Data-Cleaning Algorithms
related_work.authors: Patricia C. Arocena, Boris Glavic, Giansalvatore Mecca, Renee J. Miller, Paolo Papotti, Donatello Santoro
related_work.relation: Provides the BART error generation framework used for controlled error injection in our experiments. Also provides the canonical Tax benchmark dataset.

related_work.title: REIN: A Comprehensive Benchmark Framework for Data Cleaning Methods in ML Pipelines
related_work.authors: Mohamed Abdelaal, Christian Hammacher, Harald Schoening
related_work.relation: Provides comprehensive benchmark datasets (including Beers) and evaluation methodology for data cleaning methods. We source the Beers benchmark dataset from this repository.

hypothesis: A Constraint Interaction Graph that captures repair safety relationships between heterogeneous constraint pairs can be efficiently computed and used to decompose multi-constraint data repair into independent subproblems, achieving repair quality (F1) within 5% of holistic methods while providing 3-10x speedup proportional to the degree of interaction sparsity in the constraint set. Under minimum-change update repair semantics with unique solutions, the decomposition is provably equivalent to monolithic repair when the CIG is acyclic.
success_criteria: CIG-based decomposition produces at least 2 independent components on all real-world benchmark constraint sets (Hospital, Tax, Flights, Beers), confirming that interaction sparsity exists in practice
CRIS repair F1 is within 5% of the strongest holistic baseline on Hospital, Tax, Flights, and Beers benchmarks
CRIS achieves at least 2x runtime speedup over Greedy-Holistic on constraint sets with >15 constraints
Structural safety analysis (Level 1) identifies >60% of constraint pairs as safe on real datasets
CRIS introduces fewer cascading violations than sequential independent repair (measured by cascade count metric)
Epsilon sensitivity analysis shows smooth F1 trade-off: <1% F1 degradation as epsilon increases from 0.001 to 0.01

computational_requirements.justification: All computation is constraint checking (polynomial in data size), graph analysis (polynomial in constraint count, typically <50), and composition of existing repair algorithms. No neural network training. Dataset acquisition ~30min, constraint discovery ~30min, CIG construction ~20min, CRIS repair experiments ~2h, baseline comparisons ~1.5h, HoloClean on Hospital ~30min, scalability experiments ~1h, epsilon sensitivity ~30min, synthetic experiments ~1h. Total ~7.5h within 8h budget. We use actual benchmark datasets from HoloClean, BART, and REIN repositories rather than synthetic mimics. Datasets range from ~1K tuples (Hospital) to ~200K tuples (Tax).
